examples.title: Memory Read Operation
examples.description: Demonstrates how the CPU reads data from a memory location
examples.explanation: The CPU places the memory address on the address bus, asserts the READ signal, waits for memory to fetch the data, and reads the data from the data bus into an internal register.

examples.title: Memory Write Operation
examples.description: Demonstrates how the CPU writes data to a memory location
examples.explanation: The CPU places both the target address and the data to be written on their respective buses, asserts the WRITE signal, waits for memory to complete the storage operation.

examples.title: Stack PUSH and POP Operations
examples.description: Demonstrates stack memory management with PUSH and POP
examples.explanation: PUSH decrements the stack pointer and stores data. POP retrieves data and increments the stack pointer. Used for managing function calls, saving/restoring registers, and implementing recursion.

examples.title: Demonstrating Endianness
examples.description: Shows how data is stored in memory for little-endian and big-endian systems
examples.code:
import struct

# Integer to store
value = 0x12345678

# Convert to bytes (4 bytes)
bytes_data = value.to_bytes(4, byteorder='big')

print(f"Value: 0x{value:08X}")
print(f"As bytes (big-endian): {bytes_data.hex()}")

# Show little-endian representation
bytes_little = value.to_bytes(4, byteorder='little')
print(f"As bytes (little-endian): {bytes_little.hex()}")

# Memory address representation
print("\n--- Memory Layout ---")
print("Address  Big-Endian  Little-Endian")
for i in range(4):
    print(f"  {i}        {bytes_data[i]:02X}           {bytes_little[i]:02X}")

# Output shows:
# Big-endian: MSB at lowest address
# Little-endian: LSB at lowest address
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates endianness by showing how the 4-byte integer 0x12345678 is stored in memory. Big-endian stores 12 at address 0, 34 at address 1, etc. Little-endian stores 78 at address 0, 56 at address 1, etc.
